title: parablechnum stipitellatum
intro: Parablechnum stipitellatum, a type of fern native to New Zealand, is a non-vascular plant that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. The medicinal properties of Parablechnum stipitellatum include its antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Parablechnum stipitellatum is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to support its medicinal properties.
